title: witheringia laxissima
intro: Witheringia laxissima, also known as the 'Chillakada' or 'Gandhak', is a perennial plant species native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones in its leaves and roots. The health benefits of Witheringia laxissima include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
